title: Method for downlink channel estimation
abstract: The present invention relates to a method for downlink channel estimation in a base station of a Multiple-Input Multiple-Output wireless communication system, wherein a base station of the Multiple-Input Multiple-Output wireless communication system has N T antennas and N FFT sub-carriers, the method comprising: precoding a pilot signal originated from each radio frequency link by means of a first analog precoding matrix associated with the each radio frequency link to obtain a first pilot signal; and mapping the precoded first pilot signal to all antennas connected to the each radio frequency link, and transmitting the precoded first pilot signal by the antennas.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A method for downlink channel estimation in a base station of a Multiple -Input Multiple-Output wireless communication system, wherein a base station of the Multiple-Input Multiple-Output wireless communication system has N T antennas and 횓혶횕혖횕혖횕혖 sub-carriers, the method comprising: - precoding a pilot signal originated from each radio frequency link by means of a first analog precoding matrix associated with the each radio frequency link to obtain a first pilot signal; and - mapping the precoded first pilot signal to all antennas connected to the each radio frequency link, and transmitting the precoded first pilot signal by the antennas. 2. The method according to claim 1, wherein pilot signals originated from different radio frequency links are transmitted by means of different OFDM symbols. 3. The method according to claim 1, wherein the precoded first pilot signal is transmitted concurrently by all antennas connected to the each radio frequency link. 4. The method according to claim 1, wherein a number S of the radio frequency links used by the base station is less than a number N T of the antennas. 5. The method according to claim 4, wherein the base station selects s창혟짚S radio frequency links from the number S of the used radio frequency links for transmitting pilot signals. 6. The method according to claim 1, further comprising: - partitioning a time-frequency resource including T OFDM symbols and 횓혶횕혖횕혖횕혖 subcarriers to obtain time-frequency resource blocks including the same number of time-frequency resource units; - precoding a pilot signal in each time-frequent resource block using a second analog precoding matrix associated with the time-frequency resource block to obtain a second pilot signal; and - mapping the precoded second pilot signal to all antennas connected to the each radio frequency link, and transmitting the precoded second pilot signal by the antennas. 7. The method according to claim 6, wherein respective rows, in the second analog precoding matrix, corresponding to different antennas, are mutually orthogonal. 8. A base station for downlink channel estimation in a Multiple -Input Multiple-Output wireless communication system, wherein the base station is characterized in implementing the method according to any one of claims 1-7. 9. A method for downlink channel estimation in a user equipment of a Multiple-Input Multiple-Output wireless communication system, the method comprising: - receiving a first pilot signal from a base station connected to the user equipment, wherein the first pilot signal is originated from each radio frequency link, is precoded by virtue of the first 

 analog precoding matrix associated with the each radio frequency link, is mapped to all antennas connected to the each radio frequency link and transmitted by the antennas; and - performing, by the user equipment, delay estimation according to the first pilot signal. 10. The method according to claim 9, further comprising: - receiving a second pilot signal from a base station connected to the user equipment, wherein the second pilot signal is derived by partitioning a time-frequency resource including T OFDM symbols and 횓혶횕혖횕혖횕혖 sub-carriers to obtain time-frequency resource blocks including the same number of time-frequency resource units, precoding the pilot signal in each time-frequency resource block using a second analog precoding matrix associated with the time-frequency resource block, being mapped to all antennas connected to the each radio frequency link, and transmitted by the antennas; and - performing, by the user equipment, channel estimation according to the second pilot signal. 11. The method according to claim 10, wherein the user equipment further facilitates channel estimation based on the delay estimation. 12. A user equipment for downlink channel estimation in a Multiple-Input Multiple-Output wireless communication system, wherein the base station is characterized in implementing the method according to any one of claims 9-11.
